label: Wage and salaried workers
source: World Bank WDI / ILOSTAT modelled estimate
explanation: Shows how much of the labor market is formal wage or salaried work, so the product can avoid over-promising formal jobs where mixed livelihoods dominate.

label: Individuals using the Internet
source: World Bank WDI / ITU World Telecommunication/ICT Indicators
explanation: Digital access affects whether ICT support, blended training, platform work, or remote support are realistic opportunities.

label: Employment in services
source: World Bank WDI / ILOSTAT modelled estimate
explanation: Services employment supports realistic pathways in repair, retail, customer-facing work, ICT support, and local service businesses.

label: Employment in agriculture
source: World Bank WDI / ILOSTAT modelled estimate
explanation: Agriculture remains a major employment sector, so agricultural pathways are realistic when the worker has crop, field, coordination, or record-keeping skills.

label: Wage and salaried workers
source: World Bank WDI / ILOSTAT modelled estimate
explanation: Formal wage work is a smaller share of employment, so the system should surface self-employment, local services, and training pathways alongside jobs.

label: Individuals using the Internet
source: World Bank WDI / ITU World Telecommunication/ICT Indicators
explanation: Connectivity is strong enough to include digital extension, phone-based training, and some remote or platform-enabled services, while rural access gaps still matter.

label: Employment in services
source: World Bank WDI / ILOSTAT modelled estimate
explanation: Services employment indicates reachable opportunities in local support, retail, logistics, digital assistance, and small enterprise work.

label: Employment in agriculture
source: World Bank WDI / ILOSTAT modelled estimate
explanation: Agriculture is still a large employment sector, so rural matching should include farm services, extension support, records, cooperative work, and adjacent training.

label: Wage and salaried workers
source: World Bank WDI / ILOSTAT modelled estimate
explanation: The formal wage share is meaningful but limited, so opportunity matching should blend formal jobs, informal work, apprenticeships, and enterprise pathways.

label: Individuals using the Internet
source: World Bank WDI / ITU World Telecommunication/ICT Indicators
explanation: Connectivity constraints should shape how strongly the tool recommends online training, remote support, or platform work.

label: Employment in services
source: World Bank WDI / ILOSTAT modelled estimate
explanation: A large service sector supports pathways in local services, customer-facing work, repair, digital support, and small businesses.

label: Employment in agriculture
source: World Bank WDI / ILOSTAT modelled estimate
explanation: Agriculture is a major employment base, so agricultural, cooperative, and extension-linked opportunities are locally realistic.

label: Wage and salaried workers
source: World Bank WDI / ILOSTAT modelled estimate
explanation: The wage-work share helps calibrate the balance between formal jobs, factory work, informal work, and training pathways.

label: Individuals using the Internet
source: World Bank WDI / ITU World Telecommunication/ICT Indicators
explanation: Digital access supports blended training and digital-adjacent work, but recommendations should still account for uneven connectivity.

label: Employment in services
source: World Bank WDI / ILOSTAT modelled estimate
explanation: Services employment supports matching into local services, retail, logistics, customer support, and digital assistance.

label: Employment in agriculture
source: World Bank WDI / ILOSTAT modelled estimate
explanation: Agriculture remains a large employment base, so rural pathways should include crop work, records, cooperative work, and adjacent training.

label: Wage and salaried workers
source: World Bank WDI / ILOSTAT modelled estimate
explanation: A low formal wage share means the matching layer must treat self-employment, apprenticeships, informal work, and training as first-class opportunities.

label: Individuals using the Internet
source: World Bank WDI / ITU World Telecommunication/ICT Indicators
explanation: Connectivity is sufficient for some digital pathways but still uneven enough that offline and low-bandwidth options should remain central.

label: Employment in services
source: World Bank WDI / ILOSTAT modelled estimate
explanation: A large service sector supports reachable pathways in local services, repair, retail, customer support, logistics, and small enterprise work.

label: Employment in agriculture
source: World Bank WDI / ILOSTAT modelled estimate
explanation: Agriculture remains a significant employment sector, so field work, agribusiness support, and adjacent training can be realistic.
